name: Frozen Bread Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine 2 1/2 cups flour and yeast.
2. In a saucepan, heat milk, margarine, sugar, and salt just until warm (115 to 120°F), stirring constantly until margarine almost melts.
3. Add the liquid mixture to the dry ingredients in the mixing bowl along with eggs. Beat at low speed for 1/2 minute, scraping sides of the bowl constantly.
4. Beat for 3 minutes at high speed.
5. Stir in the remaining flour with a spoon until you can mix it in with your hands. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface.
6. Knead the dough into a stiff mixture that is smooth and elastic (about 6 to 8 minutes total).
7. Divide the dough into thirds. Shape each of the two portions into loaves.
8. Store in a freezer container with a double thickness of waxed paper between.
9. Divide the remaining dough into 16 balls. Arrange the balls about an inch apart in a freezer container.
